result:
Implemented browser-side search parity for notes in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go) and added coverage in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go).

The web UI now supports:
- Case-insensitive full-text search across note titles and bodies.
- Tag filtering with multiple tags, matching notes that contain all selected tags.
- Archived modes matching the CLI behavior: hide archived, include archived, and archived only.

I wired those filters through the sidebar on `/` and related note pages, preserved active filters in sidebar note links, and added result snippets from the note body so search results are usable in the browser. I also kept tag chips working by turning them into additive/removable multi-tag filters instead of a single-tag toggle.

Tests were extended for:
- Search matching body text plus multi-tag filtering.
- Archived-only search results.
- Presence of the new search form in the UI.

I could not run `gofmt` or `go test` here because this environment does not have `go`/`gofmt` installed on `PATH`.
